content.problemStatement:
Chef has two ranges [A,B][A, B][A,B] and [C,D][C, D][C,D]. Chef needs to find the number of integers that belong to **at least** one of the ranges.

Note: A range [P,Q][P, Q][P,Q] contains **all** the integers {P,P+1,P+2,…,Q−1,Q}\{P, P+1, P+2, \dots, Q-1, Q\}{P,P+1,P+2,…,Q−1,Q}.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing 444 integers A,B,C,A, B, C,A,B,C, and DDD, as mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of integers that belong to at least one of the ranges.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers A B C D describing the two ranges [A, B] and [C, D].
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of distinct integers that belong to at least one of the two ranges.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: ranges [1,3] and [5,5] have no overlap, union size = 3+1=4.
Test case 2: ranges [3,8] and [2,5] overlap on {3,4,5}, union size = 6+4-3=7.
Test case 3: ranges [1,8] and [4,6] overlap on {4,5,6}, union size = 8+3-3=8.
Test case 4: both ranges are the single integer 5, union size = 1.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the size of the first range (len1 = B‑A+1) and the second range (len2 = D‑C+1). The overlap length is max(0, min(B,D)‑max(A,C)+1). The answer is len1 + len2 – overlap. This runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        A = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it)); C = int(next(it)); D = int(next(it))
        len1 = B - A + 1
        len2 = D - C + 1
        start = max(A, C)
        end = min(B, D)
        overlap = max(0, end - start + 1)
        out.append(str(len1 + len2 - overlap))
    sys.stdout.write('
'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
